behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summaries can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization quality (who said what), not just wrapper code. A stubbed STT response would hide label-loss errors. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model boundary (STT label preservation), not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known utterances/attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/transformation logic on transcript text into a schema object. The failure boundary is local extraction logic, not OS/network/model behavior. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module handoff to manifest the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures with explicit assignments, assert parser output contains correct assignee-task pairs and excludes non-assignment statements.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM generation/completeness over attendee-task coverage; deterministic mocks would always pass formatting checks while missing real omission failures. Rejected Workflow: a single summarization decision boundary is sufficient to test this requirement; multi-step agent chain is not explicitly required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section, not generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'identified decisions' then 'routes' to schema fields, which is deterministic mapping logic. No real infra or model judgment is needed if decision items are already identified. Rejected Integration: no required module-boundary failure is described.
behaviors.test_description: Provide pre-identified decision objects to routing logic and assert they are emitted only in `decisions` fields and never in narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions in transcript appear in the live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality/placement behavior from real LLM summarization (decision salience + section placement). Stubbed outputs cannot reveal model misses where decisions are omitted or misplaced. Rejected Workflow: behavior focuses on one live summary outcome, not explicitly multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript samples with known decisions through the live summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service splits into segments, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequenced multi-stage processing (split -> process each segment -> merge) and final coherence, which depends on chained model outputs across steps. Single-step tests miss cross-step drift/merge failures. Rejected System-only: real OS/network may be involved, but the defining failure boundary is multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a >60-minute real recording, execute end-to-end segmented processing with real model calls, and assert segment order is preserved, outputs are merged, and final summary remains globally consistent across segments.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end with real recording and multiple dependent AI steps (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery. Real failures emerge from step-to-step handoff and cumulative behavior, which lower-level or single-step tests can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job on a real meeting file and verify autonomous completion plus valid structured output containing required sections and schema.
